title: Unexpected default LogDriver (and CgroupManager?) on rootless containers when PID listings are hidden (hidepid > 0)
body:
This follows [this discussion](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/26709).

The default LogDriver election indirectly depends on [`func useSystemd()`](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/common/pkg/config/systemd.go#L53). It reads `/proc/1/comm` and expects its value to equal `systemd`.

Troubles arise when the host system is hardened to [hide PID listings from non-root users](https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/hidepid-linux-hide-pid). On such system, rootless containers end up with a different LogDriver, despite Systemd and Journald being fully supported. 

`podman-logs` knows how to find its logs, so this may not cause immediate issue. However, with Quadlet containers, which are defacto managed by Systemd, units logs aren’t accessible through `journalctl --user` (messages’ `_UID` are set to containers namespaced user ids; not actual user id).

An easy workaround is to specify `LogDriver=journald` in the quadlet files.

`func useSystemd()` is also called in [`func defaultCgroupManager()`](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/common/pkg/config/systemd.go#L28), I wonder if this could lead to other side effects?

comments.body:
The check is just wrong, the proper way per systemd docs is to check /run/systemd/system which we already do here https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/4c84c257562b37a8a106c439b351118b2313179e/common/pkg/systemd/systemd_linux.go#L24-L31

So we just have to use the same code there
Ok, I’ll update the PR then!
